questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the husband of Sita?
questions.explanation: Sita is the devoted consort of Rama, the prince of Ayodhya, in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which king of Mithila raised Sita as his daughter?
questions.explanation: Sita was raised by King Janaka of Mithila, and so she is often called Janaki.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, how was the infant Sita first found?
options: In a furrow of the earth while ploughing
questions.explanation: Sita was discovered by King Janaka in a furrow of the field as he ploughed the earth, which is why she is called the daughter of the Earth.

questions.q: Sita is honored in tradition as an incarnation of which goddess?
questions.explanation: Sita is revered as an incarnation of the goddess Lakshmi, the eternal consort of Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which king of Lanka abducts Sita and carries her away?
questions.explanation: Sita is abducted by Ravana, the ten-headed king of Lanka, in the Ramayana.

questions.q: To which island kingdom is Sita taken after her abduction in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sita is held captive in Ravana's kingdom of Lanka in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which devotee of Rama leaps across the ocean to Lanka and first finds Sita in captivity?
questions.explanation: Hanuman leaps across the ocean to Lanka and discovers Sita in the Ashoka grove.

questions.q: Sita is the central female figure of which great Sanskrit epic?
questions.explanation: Sita is the heroine of the Ramayana, the epic traditionally attributed to the sage Valmiki.

questions.q: When Rama is exiled to the forest, what does Sita choose to do in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Out of devotion, Sita chooses to follow Rama into his fourteen-year forest exile rather than stay behind in comfort.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which brother of Rama accompanies Rama and Sita into the forest exile?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, Rama's devoted younger brother, accompanies Rama and Sita throughout their exile.

questions.q: Because she was raised by King Janaka, by what patronymic name is Sita commonly known?
questions.explanation: Sita is widely called Janaki, meaning the daughter of Janaka.

questions.q: Sita is also called Maithili because she is associated with which kingdom?
questions.explanation: The name Maithili refers to Sita as the princess of Mithila, the realm of King Janaka.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, how does Rama win the hand of Sita at the swayamvara in Mithila?
options: By defeating Janaka in single combat
By lifting and stringing Shiva's mighty bow
questions.explanation: Rama wins Sita by lifting and stringing the great bow of Shiva, a feat no other suitor could accomplish.

questions.q: The bow that Rama lifts to win Sita in the Ramayana belonged to which deity?
questions.explanation: The great bow that Rama strings to win Sita is the celebrated bow of Shiva kept in Janaka's court.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what golden creature does Sita long for, leading to Rama's pursuit and her abduction?
questions.explanation: Sita is enchanted by a golden deer, the demon Maricha in disguise, whose pursuit by Rama allows Ravana to abduct her.

questions.q: Which demon disguises himself as the golden deer that lures Rama away from Sita?
questions.explanation: The demon Maricha takes the form of the golden deer to deceive Sita and draw Rama away in the Ramayana.

questions.q: In which grove of Lanka is Sita held captive by Ravana in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sita is kept under guard in the Ashoka grove (Ashoka Vatika) within Ravana's Lanka.

questions.q: What token does Hanuman carry from Rama to assure Sita of his identity in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman shows Sita Rama's signet ring as a sign that he is truly Rama's messenger.

questions.q: After Ravana is defeated, what ordeal does Sita undergo to affirm her purity in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sita undergoes the Agni Pariksha, an ordeal by fire, from which she emerges unharmed, affirming her constancy.

questions.q: Which fire-god, invoked during Sita's ordeal in the Ramayana, attests to her purity?
questions.explanation: Agni, the god of fire, bears witness to Sita's purity during the fire ordeal in the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Sita is traditionally regarded as the daughter of which goddess, to whom she finally returns?
questions.explanation: Sita is regarded as the daughter of the goddess Earth, Bhudevi, having arisen from the furrow, and the Ramayana relates that she finally returns to her.

questions.q: In which forest settlement do Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana dwell, from where Sita is later abducted?
questions.explanation: Sita is abducted from the hermitage at Panchavati in the Dandaka forest where the three were dwelling.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who are the twin sons born to Sita during her later forest dwelling?
questions.explanation: Sita gives birth to the twins Lava and Kusha in the hermitage of the sage Valmiki.

questions.q: In whose hermitage does Sita take refuge and raise her twin sons in the later Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sita dwells in the hermitage of the sage Valmiki, who is traditionally regarded as the author of the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which aged vulture-king tries to rescue Sita as Ravana carries her away?
questions.explanation: The noble vulture Jatayu fights Ravana to save Sita and is mortally wounded in the attempt.

questions.q: What is the name of the rakshasi in the Ashoka grove who comforts Sita and recounts a dream foretelling Ravana's fall?
questions.explanation: Trijata, a rakshasi in the Ashoka grove, is sympathetic to Sita and recounts a dream foretelling Ravana's fall.

questions.q: Ravana's chief queen, who pleads with him to return Sita, is named whom?
questions.explanation: Mandodari, the chief queen of Ravana, urges her husband to return Sita to Rama in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Because she arose from the earth and not from a human womb, Sita is described by which term?
options: Ayonija, not born of a womb
questions.explanation: Sita is called ayonija, one not born from a womb, since she arose from a furrow of the earth.

questions.q: In the popular Ramayana tradition, the protective boundary Lakshmana draws around Sita's dwelling is known as what?
questions.explanation: In the popular tradition, the protective line drawn by Lakshmana for Sita's safety is called the Lakshmana Rekha.

questions.q: Sita is venerated as the embodiment of which qualities in the Ramayana tradition?
questions.explanation: Sita is honored as the embodiment of devotion, constancy, and steadfast virtue throughout the Ramayana tradition.
